articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Nathan Tella (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Emre Can (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Niclas Füllkrug (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, Borussia Dortmund 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Marius Wolf replaces Ian Maatsen.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Marco Reus replaces Jadon Sancho.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Florian Wirtz replaces Nathan Tella because of an injury.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Felix Nmecha replaces Emre Can.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0. Niclas Füllkrug (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Marcel Sabitzer with a cross.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Julien Duranville replaces Jamie Bynoe-Gittens.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Salih Özcan replaces Julian Brandt.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Patrik Schick replaces Jonas Hofmann.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Victor Boniface replaces Piero Hincapié.
Nico Schlotterbeck (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card.
Jeremie Frimpong (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Granit Xhaka (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1. Josip Stanisic (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from the left side of the six yard box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Florian Wirtz with a cross   following a corner.
Second Half ends, Borussia Dortmund 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Josip Stanisic nods home Short Goals vs. Borussia Dortmund) 2024/04/21 ESHEET

article.story:
Bayer Leverkusen's Josip Stanisic scored a stoppage-time goal for the champions to rescue a 1-1 Bundesliga draw at Borussia Dortmund on Sunday and prevent Xabi Alonso's side from suffering their first loss of the season.

Striker Niclas Fullkrug gave Dortmund the lead in the 81st minute with a volley inside the left post, after Marcel Sabitzer teed him up with a lovely cushioned pass before the ball went in off goalkeeper Lukas Hradecky's gloves.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

But Stanisic levelled for Leverkusen in the seventh minute of stoppage time, heading home a Florian Wirtz corner to extend the newly-crowned champions' unbeaten run in all competitions to 45 games.

"The goal we conceded hurt, but we kept going and I'm happy with our reaction," Alonso told DAZN. "We're still waiting for our big party. Now we'll recover a bit and then prepare well for Stuttgart, which will be a very exciting game.

"We just don't want to lose. The goal was very emotional, celebrating with the fans was very nice and it means a lot to us all."

Leverkusen, who last weekend secured their first Bundesliga title with a 5-0 victory over Werder Bremen, are on 80 points from 30 games.

Dortmund, who on Tuesday advanced to the Champions League semifinals with a 5-4 aggregate win over Atletico Madrid, are fifth with 57 points, two points behind fourth-placed Leipzig.

Nathan Tella could have then opened the scoring for Alonso's men from point-blank range in the 68th minute, after Jonas Hofmann squared him the ball inside the box, but Dortmund keeper Gregor Kobel showed off his skills to catch the ball on the goalline.

Things got tense between the players and Leverkusen forward Victor Boniface was initially shown a red card for what seemed an unsportsmanlike conduct, but the referee overturned his decision after a VAR check.

Grimaldo had another great chance from a free kick as he sent the ball towards the upper corner that was denied by a stunning save from Kobel, before Stanisic found the record-saving equaliser less than a minute later following a corner.

Leverkusen have not won any of their last four Bundesliga matches against Dortmund, their longest ongoing winless run against any current top-flight side.
